note_data.title: Respiration
keyPoints: **Respiration** is the chemical breakdown of glucose to release energy in all living cells
**Aerobic respiration** requires oxygen and produces carbon dioxide, water and ATP
**Anaerobic respiration** occurs without oxygen and produces lactic acid (animals) or ethanol and carbon dioxide (plants/yeast)
Aerobic respiration produces **38 ATP molecules** per glucose molecule compared to only **2 ATP molecules** in anaerobic respiration
**Mitochondria** are the site of aerobic respiration in cells
During vigorous exercise, **oxygen debt** occurs when muscles respire anaerobically due to insufficient oxygen supply
**Fermentation** is anaerobic respiration in yeast used in bread making and alcohol production
Aerobic respiration is **more efficient** than anaerobic respiration because it releases more energy per glucose molecule

definitions.definition: The chemical breakdown of glucose to release energy in living cells
The breakdown of glucose using oxygen to release energy
The breakdown of glucose without oxygen to release energy
The amount of oxygen needed to break down lactic acid produced during anaerobic respiration

howToAnswer.instruction: Give one fact in one sentence. No explanation needed.
Give the point + reason using 'because' or 'this is because'
Give at least two differences between aerobic and anaerobic respiration
Give a sequence of events or detailed account

modelAnswer.question: Explain why aerobic respiration is more efficient than anaerobic respiration. [3 marks]
modelAnswer.answer: Aerobic respiration produces 38 ATP molecules per glucose molecule while anaerobic respiration produces only 2 ATP molecules per glucose molecule. This is because aerobic respiration completely breaks down glucose using oxygen, while anaerobic respiration only partially breaks down glucose without oxygen.
markBreakdown: Mark 3: Explanation of complete vs partial breakdown

commonMistakes: Never say 'breathing' when the question asks about respiration - breathing is gas exchange, respiration is energy release
Students often confuse products: aerobic produces CO₂ + H₂O, anaerobic in animals produces lactic acid only
Never say anaerobic respiration produces CO₂ in animals - this only happens in plants/yeast
Students forget that both types of respiration break down glucose - they don't use different substances
Don't say oxygen is 'used up' - say oxygen is 'required' or 'needed'
examinerTips: This topic appears in almost every paper - learn both word and symbol equations
Always specify whether describing anaerobic respiration in animals or plants/yeast
Use numbers for ATP production - examiners want specific values
Draw and label mitochondria diagram for cellular respiration questions
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Mitochondrion structure showing outer membrane, inner membrane with cristae, and matrix where respiration occurs. Also include comparison table of aerobic vs anaerobic respiration products.
